title: "It's Up to You" with Charles Brannan
Description: One of a series of radio programs called "It's Up to You," hosted by the Women's Democratic Committee. It is hosted by India Edwards, executive director of the women's section of the Democratic party, with guest Charles Brannan, US Secretary of Agriculture. The subject of this 1952 election political program is farming.
Speakers: Brannan, Charles F. (Charles Franklin), 1903-1992; Edwards, India, 1895-1990
Internal Note: Recorded by Audio-video recording company, Inc. 730 5th Ave, NYC. ABC Recording. WJZ (Radio station : New York, N.Y.)

title: "It's Up to You" with Eleanor Roosevelt
Description: From a series of radio programs called "It's Up to You," sponsored by the Women's Democratic Committee. Hosted by India Edwards, executive director of the women's division of the Democratic party. The guest is Eleanor Roosevelt, former First Lady of the United States, diplomat, and activist. The subject is Democratic Women's Day, when women were admitted to the Executive Committee of the Democratic Party in 1919, and also women taking part in government.

title: Democratic Record Show
Description: Senator Kenneth Wherry's statement about doing away with meat price controls and the OPA wins the "headache to housewives" prize. India Edwards, Executive Director, Women's Division of the Democratic National Committee, make remarks on a political program by the Democratic National Committee called the Democratic Record Show. Originally two discs; 12 inches, in three parts.
Related Collection: Edwards, India Papers
Preservation: Copied onto reel-to-reel tape.
Internal Note: Recorded American Broadcasting Company

title: Democratic Record Show
Description: Gladys Dickason, a union leader, makes remarks on a political program by the Democratic National Committee called the Democratic Record Show. Originally two discs, 12 inches, in three parts.
Preservation: Copied onto reel-to-reel tape.
Internal Note: Recorded by American Broadcasting Company

title: Democratic Record Show
Description: Dorothy Vredenburgh, Secretary of the Democratic National Committee, makes remarks on a political program by the Democratic National Committee called the Democratic Record Show. Governor Thomas Dewey makes brief remarks as well. Originally two discs, 12 inches, in three parts.
Speakers: Bush, Dorothy Vredenburgh, 1916-1991; Dewey, Thomas E. (Thomas Edmund), 1902-1971
Related Collection: Edwards, India Papers
Preservation: Copied onto reel-to-reel tape.

title: Merle Miller Interview with Harry S. Truman, Tape 8, Side B
Description: Conversation among Harry S. Truman, Merle Miller, David Noyes, and William Hillman, Tape 8, Side B. These tapes were the basis of the book "Plain Speaking." See finding aid for Merle Miller tapes for detailed information and description. This recording deals with such subjects as: 1940 Senatorial campaign; Interstate Commerce Commission; Mr. Truman's mother; extensive discussion of the Korean War and the need for a TV program to give the facts about it; General Douglas MacArthur including the meeting at Wake Island; civilian control of the military; stories of the Mexican War, the War of 1812, and the Civil War; prisoners of war; the United Nations; and the term "police action."
Speakers: Hillman, William, 1895-1962; Noyes, David Matthew, 1898-1981; Truman, Harry S., 1884-1972
Keywords: American Civil War, 1861-1865; Generals; Korean War, 1950-1953; Mothers; Political campaigns
People Mentioned: Acheson, Dean, 1893-1971; Eisenhower, Dwight D. (Dwight David), 1890-1969; MacArthur, Douglas, 1880-1964; Truman, Martha Ellen, 1852-1947
Related Collection: Miller, Merle Papers

title: Merle Miller Interview Harry S. Truman, Tape 2, Side B
Description: Conversation among Harry S. Truman, Merle Miller, David Noyes, and William Hillman, Tape 2, Side B. These tapes were the basis of the book "Plain Speaking." See the Merle Miller Papers finding aid for detailed information and description. This recording deals with such subjects as: the writing of the Constitution; American government; weak Presidents; memories of William Jennings Bryan.
People Mentioned: Arthur, Chester Alan, 1829-1886; Bryan, William Jennings, 1860-1925; Cleveland, Grover, 1837-1908; Garfield, James A. (James Abram), 1831-1881; Grant, Ulysses S., 1822-1885; Hayes, Rutherford Birchard, 1822-1893
Related Collection: Miller, Merle Papers
Preservation: The reel-to-reel tapes sent to us by the Johnson Library are at 3-3/4 ips.  The Truman Library made in-house duplicate reel-to-reel tapes at 7-1/2 ips.  The Johnson Library tapes are deteriorating.

title: Lacy Barnett's Presentation at the Korean Veterans Association
Description: Veteran Lacy Barnett speaks to a gathering of the Korean War Veterans Association about his research on the 34th Infantry's activities during the Korean War.
Related Collection: Korean War Collection
Preservation: Cassette is in good condition. It has not been duplicated.
Internal Note: Original CSKW number MM.63

title: "Smash," Robert F. Davis's Remembrance of the Korean War
Description: This cassette sounds like a re-recording of an earlier cassette, with a newer introduction by Robert F. Davis, who served in the United Nations Civil Assistance Command in Korea. The original recording seems to have been made around the airing of the last episode of the TV show, M*A*S*H, where Davis recounts his time in Korea.
Related Collection: Korean War Collection
Preservation: Tape is in good physical condition, but because it's a re-recording, there is a lot of hiss and the volume is very low. No other copies exist.
Internal Note: Originally CSKW number MM.135. Side A only.

title: Les Zeller Remembers his Korean War Experience
Description: Korean War veteran Les Zeller recorded his memories of his experience in the Korean War for historian Charles Spurlin at Victoria College in Victoria, TX. Spurlin, in turn, donated this material to the Center for the Study of the Korean War.
Related Collection: Korean War Collection
Preservation: Tape is in good condition. No copies exist.
Internal Note: Originally CSKW number MM.369

title: Dan Cavanaugh Recalls His Service in the Korean War
Description: Korean War veteran Dan Cavanaugh records his memories of his Army service in the Korean War. This recording was made in response to an inquiry by historian Charles Spurlin of Victoria College in Victoria, TX. This is the second of five tapes that Cavanaugh sent to Spurlin. Spurlin, in turn, donated his materials to the Center for the Study of the Korean War.
Related Collection: Korean War Collection
Preservation: Tape is in good condition. No copies exist.
Internal Note: Original CSKW number MM.365. This tape probably does NOT relate to photographs.

title: Operation Big Switch and Korean Prison Interrogations
Description: The CD is labeled "Korean War Interrogations 1952-1953." Most of this recording is in Korean making the context of it difficult to understand. Track one is in Korean. Track two is in Korean. Track three is partially in English, explaining that the recording was made in the Yondong Tho (?) Prison just after the Korean War, during Operation Big Switch. The prison was separated into two sides, with about 2000 prisoners on each side. The recording that follows are noises and singing from the prison. [The speaker says that Operation Big Switch was in 1952, but it was in fact in 1953.] Track four is introduced in English but it mostly sounds like western music with Korean singing.
Keywords: Korean War, 1950-1953; Prisoner of war camps
Related Collection: Korean War Collection
Preservation: Four tracks on original CD. Two CD copies were donated.

title: President Truman Arrives at Waller Field in Trinidad
Description: President Harry S. Truman and his party arrive on the presidential plane Independence at Waller Field, Trinidad, BWI. They are greeted by the commanding officer, Col. John M. Schweizer, Jr. They are on a stop over on their way to Brazil. This is a recording of the Radio Trinidad broadcast of the arrival. Donated by Suzan Schweizer.
Internal Note: Digitized from 78RPM albums in-house. Albums have nonsequential sides (one album has part 1 and 3, the other has part 2 and 4)

title: Operation Price Control
Description:
WOR Electrical Transcription disc labeled, Democratic National Committee. "Operation Price Control." The story of meat with the voices of President Franklin D. Roosevelt and President Harry S. Truman. 10-2073.

Democratic radio program about price controls and the rising cost of food, produced for the 1946 congressional elections. The program explains how congressional Republicans have allowed the price of food to go up, using examples like the Jenkins and Bates amendments. Contains excerpts from President Harry S. Truman's Radio Report to the Nation Announcing the Lifting of Major Price Controls, October 14, 1946. Also contains an excerpt of President Franklin D. Roosevelt's Address at a Union Dinner, September 23, 1944. Explains how Democrats care about people first, not profits.
Speakers: Roosevelt, Franklin D. (Franklin Delano), 1882-1945; Truman, Harry S., 1884-1972
Keywords: Farmers; Food; Political campaigns; Price regulation; Rationing
Preservation: Original was on  a 16-inch transcription disc that apparently plays from the outside in. Sent to NARA lab for digitization 10/2018
Internal Note: Donated by Kathleen Donahoe of the University of Pittsburgh

title: Morton Wood, Jr., Recounts His Korean War Experience
Description: In response to a request from the Center for the Study of the Korean War, First Lieutenant Morton Wood, Jr., recorded his memories of his Korean War experience from his home in Bethesda, MD. He served as a member of Company C, Fifth Cavalry in the U.S. Army. One cassette, two sides.
Related Collection: Center for the Study of the Korean War Records
Preservation: The cassette is in good condition. A reference CD was made 11/2018.
Internal Note: Original CSKW number MM.121

title: President Truman's Remarks on Secretary of the State Byrnes' Departure for the Paris Peace Conference
Description: President Harry S. Truman's remarks at Washington National Airport on Secretary of the State James Byrnes' departure for the Paris Peace Conference. Also includes remarks by Senator Tom Connally, Secretary of State Byrnes, and Senator Arthur Vandenberg. The president's remarks start about 15:10. The NBC radio correspondent narrates the scene. The U.S. Army Band plays in the background.
Speakers: Byrnes, James F. (James Francis), 1882-1972; Connally, Tom, 1877-1963; Truman, Harry S., 1884-1972; Vandenberg, Arthur H. (Arthur Hendrick), 1884-1951
People Mentioned: Leahy, William D. (William Daniel), 1875-1959; Ross, Charles G. (Charles Griffith), 1885-1950; Steelman, Emma Zimmerman; Steelman, John Roy, 1900-1999; Vinson, Fred M., 1890-1953

title: President Truman's Remarks Upon Secretary of State Dean Acheson's Arrival From Europe
Description: President Harry S. Truman's remarks upon Secretary of State Dean Acheson's arrival from Europe, Africa, and South America, National Airport, Washington, DC. Secretary Acheson's remarks about his 17,000 mile trip also included in recording. Text of remarks not found in Public Papers.

title: President Truman's Remarks of Welcome to Queen Juliana of the Netherlands
Description: President Harry S. Truman's remarks of welcome to Queen Juliana of the Netherlands, Washington National Airport. The President spoke at 4:05 p.m. Recording also includes the Queen's response. Queen Juliana and her husband, Prince Bernhard, had arrived in Washington to begin an extended tour of the United States and Canada.
Speakers: Juliana, Queen of the Netherlands, 1909-2004; Truman, Harry S., 1884-1972

title: A Tribute to Franklin D. Roosevelt and Harry S. Truman by Betty Phillips and J.B. Hardaway
Description: Betty Phillips reports reaction to Roosevelt's death in Hollywood, then J.B. Hardaway speaks. He is referred to as "Ben Hardaway," a friend of President Truman, in the broadcast. Mr. Hardaway served with Harry S. Truman in World War I, in the 129th Field Artillery in France. Copied onto reel-to-reel tape, but the album skips at the end. Part of a KMTR radio program "Hollywood Train."
Internal Note: Uploaded mp3 audio, enhanced description, and published 9/22/2020. 1 disc; 12"

title: Merle Miller Interview with Harry S. Truman, Tape 5
Description: Conversation among Harry S. Truman, Merle Miller, David Noyes, and William Hillman, Tape 5. These tapes were the basis of the book "Plain Speaking." See finding aid for Merle Miller tapes for detailed information and description. This recording deals with such subjects as: Korean War; United Nations; world government; brainwashing; Japan; Mark Twain.
Speakers: Miller, Merle, 1919-1986; Noyes, David Matthew, 1898-1981; Truman, Harry S., 1884-1972
HST Keywords: Congo; Soviet Union; Lessons of History; Roman Empire;
